s. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items,
3
Table of Contents
net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. For example, as a result of the Alaska Airlines 737-9 accident in January 2024, the FAA investigated the 737 quality control system, including Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, Inc. (Spirit), and increased its oversight of our production and quality and safety management systems. In addition, the FAA communicated it will not approve production rate increases beyond 38 per month or additional production lines until Boeing has complied with required quality and safety standards. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the utilization, discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to monitor compliance with applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations. For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 14 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability and quality of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk
4
Table of Contents
associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments has and could continue to adversely affect product quality, production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We have also experienced significant increases in supplier prices which are adversely affecting our business. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Cautionary Note About Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "will," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and other similar words or expressions, or the negative thereof, generally can be used to help identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Forward-looking statements are based on expectations and assumptions that we believe to be reasonable when made, but that may not prove to be accurate.
Forward-looking statements are not guarantees and are subject to risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict. Many factors, including those set forth in the "Risk Factors" section below and other important factors disclosed in this report and from time to time in our other filings with the SEC, could cause actual results to differ materially and adversely from these forward-looking statements. BA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Boeing (BA)**  
**Decision Date: 2025-04-01**  

---

### 1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2025-02-03)
Boeing is a global aerospace leader with three segments:  
- **Commercial Airplanes (BCA):** Produces the 737 MAX, 767, 777, 787, and develops the 777X. Dependent on commercial airline demand.  
- **Defense, Space & Security (BDS):** Engages in military aircraft, space systems, and defense services. Competes with Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, RTX, and others.  
- **Global Services (BGS):** Provides aftermarket services across commercial and defense platforms.  

**Key structural notes:**  
- Heavily regulated (FAA, DoD); FAA currently limits 737 production rate increases beyond 38/month until quality/safety standards are met (10-K, 2025-02-03).  
- Reliant on a complex supply chain, including sole-source suppliers (10-K, 2025-02-03).  
- Significant unionized workforce (IAM 751 contract expires September 2028) with prior labor disruption (2024 strike) impacting production (10-K, 2025-02-03).  

---

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated from Supplied Evidence)
**Positive Catalysts:**  
- **F-47 Contract Win (2025-03-21):** Boeing secured a **$20 billion U.S. Air Force contract** for the next-generation F-47 fighter jet, beating Lockheed Martin. This is highlighted as a potential turnaround catalyst for the defense segment and drove a stock rally.  
- **737 MAX Production Ramp-Up Plans (2025-03-31):** Company indicates plans to ramp up 737 MAX production, though execution remains a watchpoint.  
- **Technical Breakout (2025-03-25):** Stock “broke out” on positive earnings forecasts and reduced short interest, signaling improving market sentiment.  
- **Sector Tailwinds (2025-03-26):** Global aircraft cabin lining market forecast to grow steadily (3.4% CAGR to 2034), driven by passenger traffic and aircraft deliveries.  

**Challenges & Uncertainties:**  
- **FAA Production Cap (Referenced 2025-02-03):** FAA has blocked 737 production rate increases beyond 38/month due to quality and safety concerns following the January 2024 737-9 incident.  
- **Execution Concerns (2025-03-31, 2025-03-25):** Multiple sources cite ongoing operational challenges, cost overruns, and delays in both commercial and defense programs as key investor concerns.  
- **Earnings Outlook (2025-03-21):** Web context notes EPS estimates remain negative for FY2025 (-$3.38), with gradual improvement projected thereafter.  

---

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
**Risks:**  
1. **Regulatory & Safety Oversight:** FAA’s strict oversight and production cap limit near-term commercial revenue potential. Ongoing lawsuits and whistleblower allegations (2025-03-19, 2025-03-20, 2025-03-21) suggest a persistent safety culture challenge.  
2. **Execution & Cost Overruns:** History of delays and overruns (10-K, 2025-02-03) raises doubt about meeting F-47 contract terms and delivering on production ramp-ups.  
3. **Supply Chain & Labor Vulnerabilities:** Dependence on sole-source suppliers and potential for future labor disputes (IAM 751 contract expire 2028) pose operational risks.  
4. **Competitive Pressure:** Faces “extremely competitive” markets in commercial (Airbus, China entrants) and defense (10-K, 2025-02-03).  
5. **Legal & Reputational:** Plea agreement related to 737 MAX crashes (2025-02-28) and active whistleblower lawsuits could impact financials and brand.  

**Offsets:**  
1. **Diversified Defense Backlog:** F-47 win provides long-term, high-value program stability and could boost free cash flow over time.  
2. **Service-Based Stability (BGS):** Global Services segment offers recurring revenue and leverages installed base.  
3. **Institutional Optimism:** Some analysts view the stock as undervalued with achievable growth rates (~3%) supported by commercial and defense trends (2025-03-13).  
4. **Market Tailwinds:** Rising air travel demand and increasing defense budgets globally provide structural support.  

---

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral to Slightly Positive.**  
Boeing’s franchise remains dominant in aerospace but is currently hamstrung by regulatory constraints and operational execution issues. The F-47 contract win is a meaningful positive catalyst that could improve the defense segment’s outlook and cash flow profile. However, the commercial business faces near-term growth limitations from the FAA production cap, and persistent safety/culture risks linger. The stock’s recent breakout reflects improving sentiment, but the balance of risks—particularly around execution and legal liabilities—offsets the potential upside, leading to a neutral qualitative stance. Success in ramping 737 MAX production and executing on the F-47 program would be critical to a positive inflection.

---
**Evidence Disclosure:** All developments cited are derived from the supplied text with dates. No financial estimates or multiples are inferred.

corrections.issue: Qualitative report states 'FAA currently limits 737 production rate increases beyond 38/month' but the 10-K text says the FAA 'will not approve production rate increases beyond 38 per month or additional production lines until Boeing has complied with required quality and safety standards.' The qualifier 'until Boeing has complied' is omitted, which changes the characterization from a permanent cap to a conditional one that could be lifted.
corrections.required_action: Note that the FAA cap is conditional on Boeing meeting quality/safety standards, not a fixed ceiling, as this distinction matters for forward-looking production assumptions.

corrections.issue: Qualitative report cites 'EPS estimates remain negative for FY2025 (-$3.38)' from web context dated 2025-03-21. The raw quantitative data shows TTM net income of -$11.83B and the most recent quarter (Q4 2024) EPS of -$5.45. The -$3.38 figure is a forward estimate from an external source, not a reported figure, and is not cross-validated against the supplied financials. The report should more clearly distinguish this as an external estimate rather than presenting it alongside reported data.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that -$3.38 EPS is an external analyst estimate, not a reported or internally derived figure, and note the significant gap vs. trailing EPS of -$18.30 (TTM).

corrections.issue: Qualitative report mentions 'positive earnings forecasts' as a driver of the technical breakout (2025-03-25), but the raw quantitative evidence shows consistently negative and deteriorating earnings across all 8 quarters, with TTM net income of -$11.83B and net margin worsening from -2.9% to -17.8% over the trailing period. The phrase 'positive earnings forecasts' likely refers to forward-looking analyst expectations, but this is not distinguished from the actual reported earnings trajectory, which is deeply negative.
corrections.required_action: Distinguish forward analyst optimism from the actual reported earnings trajectory, which shows persistent and worsening losses.

unsupported_claims: : The qualitative report states 'Technical Breakout (2025-03-25): Stock broke out on positive earnings forecasts and reduced short interest.' The raw evidence summary for 2025-03-25 says 'analysts are bullish on Boeing, citing positive earnings forecasts and reduced short interest.' This is sourced from a news article, not from the raw financial data, which shows no positive earnings in any of the 8 reported quarters. The claim is not unsupported per se (it is attributed to market commentary), but it could be misleading if read as a fundamental signal.

: The qualitative report states 'Institutional Optimism: Some analysts view the stock as undervalued with achievable growth rates (~3%) supported by commercial and defense trends (2025-03-13).' The raw web context for 2025-03-13 says 'a growth rate of 3% is achievable' and 'implies ro[und/up].' The report accurately reflects the source, but the 3% growth rate is an external analyst opinion, not derived from the supplied financials, which show revenue declining -30.8% YoY in the latest quarter.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's fundamental picture remains deeply challenged—negative equity of -$23.56B, eight consecutive quarters of losses, and TTM net income of -$11.83B—but the F-47 contract win and improving sentiment have lifted the stock meaningfully from prior lows, leaving the risk/reward roughly balanced at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's fundamental picture remains deeply challenged—negative equity of -$23.56B, eight consecutive quarters of losses, and TTM net income of -$11.83B—but the F-47 contract win and improving sentiment have lifted the stock meaningfully from prior lows, leaving the risk/reward roughly balanced at current levels.
key_factors: Negative shareholders' equity of -$23.56B (Q3 2024) with total liabilities of $161.26B exceeding total assets of $137.69B, representing a structurally impaired balance sheet that constrains financial flexibility.

TTM net income of -$11.83B with the most recent quarter (Q4 2024) EPS of -$5.45 and net margin deteriorating from -2.9% to -17.8% over the trailing eight quarters, indicating persistent and worsening operating losses.
F-47 contract win (2025-03-21) worth $20 billion provides a meaningful long-term defense backlog catalyst, though execution risk remains high given Boeing's history of cost overruns.

FAA production cap at 38/month for 737 MAX is conditional on Boeing meeting quality and safety standards (10-K, 2025-02-03), creating a binary upside catalyst if compliance is achieved but leaving near-term commercial revenue constrained.

HON.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: HON (2025-04-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory (Period ending 2024-09-30)**
*   **Revenue:** Demonstrated steady quarterly growth, increasing from $8.86B (2023-Q1) to $9.73B (2024-Q3). YoY growth for the latest quarter was +5.6%.
*   **Earnings:** TTM net income was $5.70B. Recent trajectory shows a deceleration, with QoQ EPS down -8.5% and YoY EPS down -4.8%.
*   **Margins:** Latest gross margin was 38.4% ($3.75B/$9.73B). Latest operating margin was 23.6% ($2.30B/$9.73B). The range for net margin over the last year was 0.151 to 0.156.
*   **Cash Flow:** Data is unavailable.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew from $59.88B to $73.49B over the shown period. Total debt increased significantly from $15.61B to $27.69B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Net margin has compressed slightly over the last year, from 0.156 to 0.151. The YoY decline in net income (-6.6%) contrasts with revenue growth (+5.6%), indicating margin pressure.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Leverage has increased materially. Debt-to-Equity ratio was 1.541 at the latest date. Total debt ($27.69B) grew 51% from the prior year ($18.35B in 2023-Q3), while total equity ($17.97B) is only 0.8% higher than its year-ago level ($17.77B).

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   The trailing PE dilution ratio of 22.91 is within its 1-year observed range of 20.8 to 25.5.
*   The Price/Book ratio of 7.263 is within its 1-year observed range of 6.9 to 8.1.
*   The Price/Sales ratio is 3.449. The Earnings Yield is 4.4%. The net margin and ROE (31.7%) are stable versus recent history.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Latest Price:** $199.58 on 2025-03-31.
*   **Quarterly Return:** The stock declined -8.7% over the prior ~63 trading days (from $218.53).
*   **Annual Return:** The stock appreciated +3.2% over the prior ~252 trading days (from $193.34).
*   **Context:** The recent quarterly price decline of -8.7% occurred alongside a -4.8% YoY EPS decline, aligning with a modest earnings deterioration.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside Quantifiers:** 1) Revenue has shown consistent growth (7 quarters, from $8.86B to $9.73B). 2) Return on Equity remains robust at 31.7%.
*   **Downside Quantifiers:** 1) Leverage ratio (Debt/Equity) increased sharply to 1.541. 2) EPS showed negative growth both QoQ (-8.5%) and YoY (-4.8%). 3) Total debt grew substantially faster than total equity over the period.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental picture is one of modest revenue growth tempered by earnings and margin pressure, accompanied by a significant increase in financial leverage. The valuation is mid-range on historical multiples. The recent -8.7% quarterly price decline reflects the negative earnings trajectory. The quantitative risk centers on continued margin compression and elevated debt levels. The quantitative reward requires a stabilization or improvement in earnings growth and margin trends to justify current valuation on a PE of 22.91. The balance sheet's increasing debt load is a key negative quantitative factor.
HON.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis: Honeywell International (HON)**
**Date of Analysis:** 2025-04-01
**Evidence Base:** Point-in-time news articles, web context, and market reports up to 2025-03-29.

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the supplied narrative, Honeywell is a diversified industrial conglomerate. Its business is strategically aligned with three major megatrends: **automation, the future of aviation, and the energy transition** (GuruFocus, 2025-02-15). The company operates in sectors including building technologies (smoke detectors), industrial safety and sensing (hydrogen detection), energy solutions (pumps, compressors), and aerospace. It is characterized as having a robust cash flow generation capability and a focus on AI-enabled, autonomous solutions for productivity, sustainability, and safety (ainvest.com, 2025-03-02; drivesncontrols.com, 2025-02-27).

### 2. Material Recent Developments
*   **Corporate Action (Split):** Honeywell is proceeding with a plan to **split into three independent publicly traded companies**, separating its Automation and Aerospace businesses among others (The Motley Fool, 2025-03-20; ainvest.com, 2025-03-02). This restructuring is described as complex and designed to unlock shareholder value and improve focus.
*   **Capital Allocation (Acquisition):** On **2025-03-04**, Honeywell announced the acquisition of **Sundyne for $2.2 billion**. This deal is intended to bolster its **Energy and Sustainability Solutions (ESS)** business. The acquisition adds complexity to the concurrent breakup process (The Motley Fool, 2025-03-20).
*   **Market Position & Exposure:** Honeywell is noted as a key player in multiple growing end-markets according to recent reports, including:
    *   **Airport Security** (expected to exceed $25.9B by 2030) - Market report, 2025-03-03.
    *   **Industrial Cyber Security** (projected CAGR 7.66% to 2032) - Market report, 2025-03-20.
    *   **Smoke Detection** (driven by regulation and smart technology) - Market report, 2025-03-29.
    *   **Hydrogen Detection** (driven by clean energy adoption) - Market report, 2025-03-29.
    *   **Industrial Services** (driven by Industry 4.0 adoption) - Market report, 2025-03-27.
*   **Valuation Context:** A narrative from **2025-03-20** noted that Honeywell's stock was trading at a valuation level the article characterized as "about as cheap as it's been since 2020."

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Execution Complexity.** The simultaneous execution of a major corporate breakup and a large corporate acquisition (**Sundyne**) creates significant operational and managerial complexity, increasing uncertainty (The Motley Fool, 2025-03-20).
*   **Risk: Regulatory & Legal.**
    *   A **lawsuit** was filed by a fired engineer on **2025-03-14** alleging wrongful termination related to company DEI programs.
    *   The **U.S. Department of Justice Antitrust Division** noted in a **2025-03-20** update that Honeywell had formerly possessed substantial horizontal overlaps in certain military helicopter engines and services that created antitrust issues, indicating legacy regulatory scrutiny in defense markets.
*   **Offset: Strategic Repositioning.** The planned breakup and Sundyne acquisition are portrayed as strategic actions to simplify the structure, enhance focus, and strengthen a key business (ESS) in growing markets like energy security (various dates, e.g., 2025-03-04, 2025-03-20).
*   **Offset: Favored End-Markets.** The company's portfolio is well-aligned with secular growth trends in automation, aviation, safety, and energy transition, as supported by multiple positive market growth forecasts cited in the evidence.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive, with elevated near-term risks.**

The provided evidence supports a positive qualitative view driven by Honeywell's strategic positioning in growth megatrends and proactive corporate restructuring aimed at unlocking value. The Sundyne acquisition directly strengthens its offerings in a key growth area. However, this assessment is tempered by the material **execution risks** inherent in managing a complex breakup while integrating a significant acquisition, as well as ongoing **legal and regulatory headwinds**. The narrative suggests the strategic rationale is sound, but the path forward will require careful navigation.

corrections.issue: Qual report states Honeywell's stock was trading at a valuation 'about as cheap as it's been since 2020' — this is a direct quote from a Motley Fool article (2025-03-20) in the raw evidence, so it is supported. However, the quant report's PE range of 20.8–25.5 over the past year and the sparse history show PE dipped to 20.8 in May 2024, which is only modestly below the current 22.9. The 'cheapest since 2020' claim cannot be verified from the supplied 1-year window alone; the PM should note this is a narrative assertion from a single source, not confirmed by the quantitative data provided.
corrections.required_action: No action required — the claim is attributed to a cited source. PM should be aware the quantitative pack's 1-year lookback does not independently confirm or refute the 'cheapest since 2020' characterization.

corrections.issue: The qual report cites 'robust cash flow generation capability' (ainvest.com, 2025-03-02) as a company characteristic. The raw quantitative evidence shows cash flow data is entirely unavailable (all periods marked 'no data'). The TTM free cash flow is listed as n/a. This narrative claim about cash flow strength has zero quantitative support in the supplied packs.
corrections.required_action: Flag to PM that the cash flow generation narrative cited in the qualitative report is unverifiable from the supplied quantitative data. The absence of cash flow data is itself a material gap given the elevated debt levels (D/E 1.541, total debt $27.69B).

corrections.issue: The qual report describes the Sundyne acquisition as '$2.2 billion' while the raw evidence summary says 'acquiring pump and compressor maker Sundyne for $2.2 billion.' The Motley Fool article (2025-03-20) also references the deal. This is consistent. However, neither report quantifies the impact of this acquisition on the balance sheet — the sharp QoQ debt increase from $23.38B (2024-06-30) to $27.69B (2024-09-30) predates the March 2025 announcement, so the acquisition's balance-sheet impact is not yet reflected in the supplied financials. The PM should understand the leverage picture will likely worsen once Sundyne closes.
corrections.required_action: Note to PM: the already-elevated D/E of 1.541 and $27.69B debt do not yet incorporate the $2.2B Sundyne acquisition announced 2025-03-04. Post-close leverage will be higher.

unsupported_claims: The qual report's claim of 'robust cash flow generation capability' (attributed to ainvest.com, 2025-03-02) is unsupported by the quantitative pack, which shows cash flow data as entirely unavailable across all periods.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Honeywell's strategic breakup into three companies and the $2.2B Sundyne acquisition are potentially value-unlocking, but the path is fraught with execution complexity. Quantitatively, the picture is mixed: steady revenue growth (+5.6% YoY) and a high ROE (31.7%) are offset by declining EPS (-4.8% YoY), margin compression, and a materially deteriorated balance sheet (D/E 1.541, debt $27.69B). At a trailing P/E of 22.9, the stock is fairly valued for a company with these headwinds, leaving little margin of safety.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Honeywell's strategic breakup into three companies and the $2.2B Sundyne acquisition are potentially value-unlocking, but the path is fraught with execution complexity. Quantitatively, the picture is mixed: steady revenue growth (+5.6% YoY) and a high ROE (31.7%) are offset by declining EPS (-4.8% YoY), margin compression, and a materially deteriorated balance sheet (D/E 1.541, debt $27.69B). At a trailing P/E of 22.9, the stock is fairly valued for a company with these headwinds, leaving little margin of safety.
key_factors: Revenue growth of +5.6% YoY in Q3 2024 with TTM revenue of $37.85B demonstrates solid top-line momentum across diversified end-markets aligned with automation, aviation, and energy transition megatrends.

Debt-to-equity of 1.541 as of Q3 2024 (debt $27.69B vs equity $17.97B) represents significant balance sheet deterioration, with debt growing 51% YoY; the $2.2B Sundyne acquisition announced 2025-03-04 will further increase leverage post-close.
EPS declined -4.8% YoY and -8.5% QoQ in Q3 2024, with net margin compressing from 0.156 to 0.151, indicating earnings quality deterioration despite top-line growth.

The planned three-way corporate breakup (Automation, Aerospace, and other businesses) announced alongside the Sundyne acquisition creates significant execution complexity and managerial distraction over the next 12-18 months.

(1)Being jointly developed and commercialized in a worldwide collaboration with AstraZeneca.
Competition and the Health Care Environment
Competition
The markets in which the Company conducts its business and the pharmaceutical industry in general are highly competitive and highly regulated. The Company's competitors include other worldwide research-based pharmaceutical companies, smaller research companies with more limited therapeutic focus, generic drug manufacturers, and animal health care companies. The Company's operations may be adversely affected by generic and biosimilar competition as the Company's products mature, as well as technological advances of competitors, industry consolidation, patents granted to competitors, competitive combination products, new products of competitors, the generic availability of competitors' branded products, and new information from clinical trials of marketed products or post-marketing surveillance. In addition, patent rights are increasingly being challenged by competitors, and the outcome can be highly uncertain. An adverse result in a patent dispute can preclude commercialization of products or negatively affect sales of existing products and could result in the payment of royalties or in the recognition of an impairment charge with respect to intangible assets associated with certain products.
Pharmaceutical competition involves a rigorous search for technological innovations and the ability to market these innovations effectively. With its long-standing emphasis on research and development, the Company is well-positioned to compete in the search for technological innovations. The Company is active in acquiring and marketing products through external alliances, such as licensing arrangements and collaborations, and has been refining its sales and marketing efforts to address changing industry conditions. However, the introduction of new products and processes by competitors may result in price reductions and product displacements, even for products protected by patents. For example, the number of compounds available to treat a particular disease typically increases over time and can result in slowed sales growth or reduced sales of the Company's products in that therapeutic category.
The highly competitive animal health business is affected by several factors including regulatory and legislative issues, scientific and technological advances, product innovation, the quality and price of the Company's products as well as competitors' products, effective promotional efforts and the frequent introduction of generic products by competitors.
Health Care Environment and Government Regulation
Global efforts toward health care cost containment continue to exert pressure on product pricing and market access. Changes to the U.S. health care system as part of health care reform, as well as increased purchasing power of entities that negotiate on behalf of Medicare, Medicaid, and private sector beneficiaries, have contributed to pricing pressure. In several international markets, government-mandated pricing actions have reduced prices of generic and patented drugs. In addition, the Company's sales performance in 2024 was negatively affected by other cost-reduction measures taken by governments and other third parties to lower health care costs. In the U.S., the Executive Branch and Congress continue to discuss legislation designed to control health care costs, including the cost of drugs. The Company anticipates all of these actions and additional actions in the future will continue to negatively affect sales and profits.
6
Table of Contents
In addressing global cost containment pressures, the Company engages in public policy advocacy with policymakers and continues to work to demonstrate that its medicines provide value to patients and to those who pay for health care. The Company advocates with government policymakers to encourage a long-term approach to sustainable health care financing that ensures access to innovative medicines and does not disproportionately target pharmaceuticals as a source of budget savings. In markets with historically low rates of health care spending, the Company encourages those governments to increase their investments and adopt market reforms in order to improve their citizens' access to appropriate health care, including medicines.
Operating conditions have become more challenging under the global pressures of competition, industry regulation and cost containment efforts. Although no one can predict the effect of these and other factors on the Company's business, the Company continually takes measures to evaluate, adapt and improve the organization and its business practices to better meet customer needs and believes that it is well-positioned to respond to the evolving health care environment and market forces.
United States
The Company faces increasing pricing pressure from managed care organizations, government agencies and programs that could negatively affect the Company's sales and profit margins, including, through (i) practices of managed care organizations, federal and state exchanges, and institutional and governmental purchasers, and (ii) federal laws and regulations related to Medicare and Medicaid, including the Medicare Prescription Drug, Improvement, and Modernization Act of 2003, the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act of 2010 (ACA), the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 (American Rescue Plan Act), and the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (IRA). Additionally, increased utilization of the 340B Federal Drug Discount Program and restrictions on the Company's ability to identify inappropriate discounts are having a negative impact on Company performance.
In the U.S., federal and state governments for many years have pursued methods to reduce the cost of drugs and vaccines for which they pay. For example, federal and state laws require the Company to pay specified rebates for medicines reimbursed by Medicaid and to provide discounts for medicines purchased by certain state and federal entities such as the Department of Defense, Veterans Affairs, Public Health Service entities and hospitals serving a disproportionate share of low income or uninsured patients.
Additionally in the U.S., consolidation and integration among health care entities is a major factor in the competitive marketplace for pharmaceutical products. Health plans and pharmacy benefit managers have been consolidating into fewer, larger entities, thus enhancing their purchasing strength and importance. Private third-party insurers, as well as governments, employ formularies to control costs by negotiating discounted prices in exchange for formulary inclusion. MRK.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: MRK (Merck & Co., Inc.)**
**Decision Date: 2025-04-01 (All evidence point-in-time prior)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Quarterly revenue shows growth with volatility. Q4 2024 revenue was $15.62B, up +6.8% YoY from $14.63B. TTM revenue is $64.17B.
*   **Earnings:** Quarterly EPS is highly volatile, including losses. Q4 2024 EPS was $1.48. YoY EPS growth of -408.3% is distorted by the Q4 2023 EPS of -$0.48. TTM net income is $17.13B.
*   **Margins:** Margins are strong. Q4 2024 gross margin was 75.5% ($11.80B/$15.62B). Operating margin was 26.7% ($4.17B/$15.62B). Net margin (TTM) is 26.7%.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew from $37.63B (Q4 2023) to $46.37B (Q4 2024), an increase of 23.2%. Total liabilities were $70.73B, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.52 ($70.73B / $46.37B).

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Evidence is mixed. The large YoY EPS growth figures (-408.3%) are meaningless due to prior-period losses. The sequential volatility in EPS (e.g., $2.14 in Q2 2024 to $1.24 in Q3 2024 to $1.48 in Q4 2024) suggests earnings are influenced by non-recurring items or volatility in line items below gross profit. **Cash flow statements are unavailable** for a direct assessment of cash conversion.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet shows growth in equity. The net margin and ROE of 36.9% (latest) indicate the business generates solid returns on equity. The debt-to-equity ratio of 1.52 indicates moderate leverage. **Note:** The provided balance sheet data shows negative reported total liabilities in some quarters (e.g., Q2 2024: -$43.65B), which is a data anomaly that cannot be interpreted.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   The stock's valuation has compressed significantly over the supplied history. The PE ratio fell from a peak of 935.6 (2024-04-01) to 13.3 (2025-03-31). This compression occurred as the net margin expanded from 0.6% to 26.7%.
*   Current valuation levels: P/E (diluted) = 13.3, P/B = 4.9, P/S = 3.6, Earnings Yield = 7.5%. The P/E is near the bottom of its 1-year range (13.0 - 937.1). The P/B is near the bottom of its range (4.7 - 8.9).

**4. Price Level and Returns Context**
*   **Price Level:** The closing price on 2025-03-31 was $89.76, which is near the 1-year low of $81.04 and well below the 1-year high of $134.63.
*   **Returns:** The stock has declined over every measured period. Quarterly return (last ~63 days) was -10.1%. The annual return (last ~252 days) was -31.9%. The price path has been consistently negative over the past four quarters.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The primary quantitative upside is valuation reversion. The stock is trading at a PE of 13.3, its lowest point in the supplied history, despite having a TTM net margin of 26.7% and an ROE of 36.9%. A re-rating toward the 1-year median PE (using the provided points, the median is ~20.3) would represent a potential +53% price appreciation, assuming stable earnings.
*   **Downside:** The primary quantitative downside is the persistent and severe negative price momentum (-31.9% annually) and the demonstrated volatility in quarterly earnings (including negative EPS quarters). If the recent strong margins prove unsustainable or if the price decline reflects a fundamental repricing not yet captured in trailing metrics, further downside is possible. The absence of cash flow data is a risk to assessing earnings durability.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative evidence presents a conflicting picture. On one hand, MRK shows strong recent profitability (26.7% net margin, 36.9% ROE) and its valuation (P/E 13.3) is at the low end of its recent range, creating a potential upside case from multiple expansion. On the other hand, the stock is in a strong downtrend (-31.9% YoY), quarterly earnings are highly volatile including losses, and cash flow data is absent, preventing a quality check on the reported profits. The risk is that the price decline is reflecting a forward reassessment of earnings durability not visible in the trailing snapshot. The balance sheet is growing but carries notable leverage. The risk/reward is quantitatively neutral to slightly positive, contingent on the assumption that recent high margins are sustainable and that the current PE multiple is an anomaly.
MRK.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: MRK (Merck & Co.)**
*Point-in-Time Evidence as of 2025-04-01*

### 1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative
Merck is a global healthcare company operating through two reportable segments: **Pharmaceutical** and **Animal Health**.
*   **Pharmaceutical Segment:** Dominated by the immuno-oncology franchise, with **Keytruda** as its primary growth driver, generating $29.5 billion in 2024 sales. The portfolio includes vaccines (notably **Gardasil**), hospital acute care products (Bridion), and a newer cardiovascular franchise (Winrevair). The 10-K (2025-02-25) details extensive label expansions for Keytruda and new product approvals like Winrevair and Capvaxive.
*   **Animal Health Segment:** A stable, complementary business offering products for livestock and companion animals, with sales of $5.9 billion in 2024.
*   **Strategic Focus:** The company emphasizes research and development, external alliances, and navigating a competitive, cost-constrained healthcare environment.

### 2. Recent Developments (Supplied Dated Text)
*   **Legal/Reputational:** Multiple securities class action lawsuits were filed against Merck in March 2025, alleging the company made misleading statements about **Gardasil demand and inventory levels in China**. Lead plaintiff deadlines are cited for April 14, 2025 (2025-03-20, 2025-03-16). A related Gardasil product liability lawsuit ruling was appealed in March 2025 (2025-03-12).
*   **Operational:** Merck has **temporarily stopped Gardasil shipments to China until mid-2025** due to weak discretionary spending and overabundant inventory at its distributor (2025-02-18). In January 2025, Gardasil received approval in China for use in males (10-K, 2025-02-25).
*   **Competitive/Regulatory:** Web context notes headwinds from the **Inflation Reduction Act (IRA)** Medicare price negotiations and general pricing pressure (10-K, 2025-02-25). News from 2025-03-25 mentions a licensing deal amid negative sector sentiment over potential tariffs.
*   **Pipeline:** A potential FDA approval for **Clesrovimab** (RSV monoclonal antibody) is noted as a future catalyst (2025-02-22).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Litigation Overhang:** The securities fraud lawsuits related to Gardasil's China demand create uncertainty regarding potential financial liabilities and reputational damage (multiple posts, March 2025).
*   **Key Franchise Dependency:** Heavy reliance on **Keytruda**, whose sales are vulnerable to competition and patent cliffs. The simultaneous **Gardasil disruption in China** is a material near-term setback for a key growth product (2025-02-18, 10-K).
*   **Systemic Pricing Pressure:** The global healthcare environment, exemplified by the **IRA price negotiation program** and high UK levies, poses a sustained margin and revenue headwind (10-K, 2025-03-21).
*   **Competitive & Market Factors:** 10-K highlights intense competition, generic/biosimilar threats, and consolidation among buyers (pharmacy benefit managers, health plans) as structural risks.

**Offsets:**
*   **Strong Oncology Franchise:** Keytruda's continued global approvals (2024-2025) and leading market position provide a robust earnings base (10-K).
*   **Pipeline & New Product Momentum:** The portfolio includes recently launched drugs (Winrevair) and a pipeline with "20 new blockbuster drugs in development" (2025-03-05), which could diversify growth drivers.
*   **Geographic Diversification & Animal Health:** Global operations and the stable Animal Health segment provide some mitigation against single-market risks.
*   **Legal Precedent in Defense:** One report notes Merck successfully defended a Gardasil product liability lawsuit in March 2025 (2025-03-11), indicating the ability to manage specific legal challenges.

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral with Cautionary Bias.**

The evidence presents a **net negative near-term narrative**. The dominant, positive story of Keytruda's expansion is overshadowed by the **clearly documented operational stumble with Gardasil in China** and the **flare-up of associated securities litigation**. These developments introduce reputational and financial uncertainty directly related to the franchise read. However, the assessment is not outright negative because: (a) the underlying oncology franchise health and pipeline potential remain described as strong in core documents, and (b) legal losses are not yet confirmed. The offset for the near-term negatives is the company's long-term strategic assets, but these are overshadowed by the supplied, visible risks as of the cutoff date.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Q4 2024 gross margin was 75.5% ($11.80B/$15.62B)' and 'Operating margin was 26.7% ($4.17B/$15.62B)'. The raw data confirms these calculations are correct. However, the report also states 'Net margin (TTM) is 26.7%' — the raw valuation ratios show net_margin=0.267, which is consistent. No issue here. But the report claims 'ROE of 36.9% (latest)' — the raw data shows roe=0.369, which is consistent. The report also states 'The net margin and ROE of 36.9% (latest) indicate the business generates solid returns on equity' — this conflates net margin and ROE into one figure. The net margin is 26.7% and ROE is 36.9%; these are separate metrics. The phrasing is slightly misleading but not materially wrong.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; minor phrasing issue only.

corrections.issue: The quant report notes 'The provided balance sheet data shows negative reported total liabilities in some quarters (e.g., Q2 2024: -$43.65B), which is a data anomaly that cannot be interpreted.' The raw data confirms Q2 2024 total_liabilities=-43.65B and Q1 2024 total_liabilities=-40.42B, as well as Q2 2023 and Q1 2023 showing negative liabilities. This is correctly flagged as a data anomaly. However, the report then uses the Q4 2024 balance sheet (total_liabilities=70.73B, total_equity=46.37B) to compute debt-to-equity of 1.52 without noting that this is one of only a few quarters with positive liability figures. The D/E ratio is valid for Q4 2024 but the report should note the data quality issue affects the consistency of this metric across the time series.
corrections.required_action: PM should be aware that the D/E ratio of 1.52 is based on a single quarter with clean data; the broader time series has significant data quality issues that prevent trend analysis of leverage.

corrections.issue: The quant report's upside case relies on 'A re-rating toward the 1-year median PE (using the provided points, the median is ~20.3) would represent a potential +53% price appreciation.' The raw valuation history has 12 data points. The median of the PE values (935.6, 916.1, 141.2, 138.3, 21.2, 21.3, 20.3, 20.5, 20.5, 20.0, 13.3, 13.3) is indeed approximately 20.3 (average of 20.3 and 20.5). However, the first two data points (935.6 and 916.1) are extreme outliers driven by near-zero earnings (net_margin=0.006). Including them inflates the 'median' conceptually — the median is robust to outliers, so 20.3 is technically correct, but the report should note that the 'normal' PE range excluding the distortion period is roughly 13-21x, making 20.3x a reasonable but not deeply discounted target. The +53% figure is arithmetically correct if earnings hold and PE reverts to 20.3.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed; the arithmetic is valid and the report appropriately conditions on 'assuming stable earnings.'

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: MRK trades at a historically low P/E of 13.3x with strong trailing margins (26.7% net, 36.9% ROE), but the valuation discount reflects legitimate structural concerns: Gardasil China disruption, securities litigation overhang, Keytruda concentration risk, and persistent negative price momentum. The risk/reward is roughly balanced—cheap enough to limit further downside, but not cheap enough to compensate for the unresolved headwinds.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: MRK trades at a historically low P/E of 13.3x with strong trailing margins (26.7% net, 36.9% ROE), but the valuation discount reflects legitimate structural concerns: Gardasil China disruption, securities litigation overhang, Keytruda concentration risk, and persistent negative price momentum. The risk/reward is roughly balanced—cheap enough to limit further downside, but not cheap enough to compensate for the unresolved headwinds.
key_factors: Valuation at multi-year low: P/E 13.3x (vs. 1-year median ~20.3x) and P/B 4.9x near the bottom of range, with price at $89.76 down 31.9% YoY and near the 1-year low of $81.04, creating a potential +53% upside on multiple reversion if earnings hold.

Gardasil China suspension and litigation: Merck temporarily halted Gardasil shipments to China until mid-2025 due to weak demand and excess inventory (2025-02-18), while multiple securities class actions were filed in March 2025 alleging misleading statements about China demand, with lead plaintiff deadlines April 14, 2025.

Earnings volatility undermines valuation confidence: Quarterly EPS is highly variable ($2.14 in Q2 2024, $1.24 in Q3 2024, $1.48 in Q4 2024), with two of the last eight quarters showing negative operating income, and no cash flow data available to verify profit quality.

Keytruda franchise dominance and pipeline optionality: Keytruda generated $29.5B in 2024 sales with continued global label expansions, while the pipeline includes 20+ potential blockbusters and near-term catalysts like Clesrovimab (RSV) FDA approval, partially offsetting concentration risk.

e narrowly focused entities. Across our business segments, we recognize hundreds of competitors worldwide and as we execute our hybrid cloud and AI strategy, we are regularly exposed to new competitors.
Our principal methods of competition are: technology innovation; performance; price; quality; brand; our breadth of capabilities, products and services; talent; client relationships and trust; the ability to deliver business value to clients; and service and support. In order to maintain leadership, we optimize our portfolio with organic and inorganic innovations and effective resource allocation. These investments not only drive current performance but will extend our innovation leadership into the future.
Our breadth and depth of expertise enables us to take different technologies and bring them together to solve the most pressing business issues of our clients. We differentiate from other providers by bringing together incumbency with enterprises, deep expertise in technology, industries and business processes, a broad go-to-market reach including an ecosystem of partners and alliances, and a strong focus on innovation. All of these attributes position IBM for accelerated growth now and prepare us for the next set of business opportunities, such as quantum computing.
1
Table of Contents
Overall, the company is the leader or among the leaders in each of our business segments. A summary of the competitive environment for each business segment is included below:
Software:
The depth, breadth, and innovation of our software offerings, coupled with our global reach, deep industry expertise and research capabilities help differentiate our offerings from our competitors. Our hybrid cloud and AI platforms allow clients to realize their digital and AI transformations across the applications, data, and environments in which they operate. The principal competitors in this segment include: Alphabet (Google), Amazon, BMC, Broadcom, Informatica, Microsoft, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP and Splunk. We also compete with smaller, niche competitors in specific geographic regions or product segments.
Consulting:
Consulting focuses on integrating skills on strategy, experience, technology and operations by domain and industry. Consulting competes in a dynamic market including consulting, systems integration, application development, application management and business process outsourcing services. Our broad-based competitors include: Accenture, Capgemini, India-based service providers, management consulting firms, the consulting practices of public accounting firms, engineering service providers, and many companies that primarily focus on local markets or niche service areas.
Infrastructure:
IBM is well positioned in the growing hybrid cloud infrastructure market, providing on-premises and cloud-based server and storage solutions. We gain advantage and differentiation through investments in higher-value capabilities, including security, scalability, and reliability, designed especially for mission-critical and AI workloads. In addition, we offer a portfolio of life-cycle services for hybrid cloud infrastructure deployment. Our principal competitors include: Dell Technologies, Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE), Intel, NetApp and Pure Storage as well as original device manufacturers (ODMs) who provide systems that are re-branded. Further, cloud service providers are leveraging innovation in technology and service delivery to compete with traditional providers and to offer additional routes to market for server and storage systems.
Financing:
Financing provides client and commercial financing, facilitating IBM clients' acquisition of hardware, software and services. Financing's ability to manage credit and residual value risk generates a competitive advantage for the company. The key competitive factors include: interest rates charged, IT product experience, client service, contract flexibility, ease of doing business, global capabilities and residual values. In client and commercial financing, Financing primarily competes with non-captive financing entities and financial institutions.
Forward-Looking and Cautionary Statements
Certain statements contained in this Form 10-K may constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 ("Reform Act"). Forward-looking statements are based on the company's current assumptions regarding future business and financial performance. These statements by their nature address matters that are uncertain to different degrees. The company may also make forward-looking statements in other reports filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), in materials delivered to stockholders and in press releases. In addition, the company's representatives may from time to time make oral forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements provide current expectations of future events based on certain assumptions and include any statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Words such as "anticipates," "believes," "expects," "estimates," "intends," "plans," "projects," and similar expressions, may identify such forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement in this Form 10-K speaks only as of the date on which it is made. Except as required by law, the company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. In accordance with the Reform Act, set forth under Item 1A. "Risk Factors" on pages 3 to 9 are cautionary statements that accompany those forward-looking statements. Readers should carefully review such cautionary statements as they identify certain important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements and from historical trends. Those cautionary statements are not exclusive and are in addition to other factors discussed elsewhere in this Form 10-K, in the company's filings with the SEC or in materials incorporated therein by reference.
2

IBM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: IBM (Decision Date 2025-04-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue & Earnings:** Latest quarterly revenue (2024 Q4) was $17.55B, up 17.3% QoQ but only +1.0% YoY. TTM revenue is $62.75B. Quarterly earnings (EPS) are volatile: Q4 2024 EPS was $3.11, following a Q3 loss of -$0.36 and prior quarters averaging below $2.00. TTM net income is $6.02B.
- **Margins:** Latest gross margin is 56.7% (Q4 2024). Operating margin averaged ~9.2% over the last year, with Q3 2024 at a negative operating margin (-5.4%). Net margin is 9.6%.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew to $27.39B (2024 Q4). Total liabilities ($109.78B) are 4x equity. Quarterly equity growth is positive from $22.61B a year prior.
- **Cash Flow:** Explicit cash flow data is unavailable for all provided quarters.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Highly inconsistent. TTM operating income is $5.80B, but includes a quarter (Q3 2024) with an operating loss of -$802M. Net income swings from -$330M (Q3) to +$2.91B (Q4). Cash flow data is missing, preventing assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion.
- **Balance Sheet Resilience:** Leverage is high. The debt-to-equity ratio (total liabilities / total equity) was 4.1x as of 2024 Q4 ($109.78B / $27.39B). Equity has grown, but asset growth relies heavily on liabilities.

**3. Valuation**
- Current P/E (diluted) is 38.67, at the high end of its 365-day window range (18.8 to 40.7). Price-to-Book is 8.51, also near its historical high of 10.0. The earnings yield is 2.6%.
- Historical context: The P/E was 18.79 one year prior (2024-05-02) and has expanded significantly, while net margin has compressed from 13.2% to 9.6% and ROE from 0.351 to 0.22 over the same period.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
- Latest price (2025-03-31): $248.66. Annual return: +30.3%. Quarterly return: +10.6%.
- The stock has rallied from a low of $162.62 to a high of $266.45 over the lookback period, trading near its 52-week high.
- Price appreciation has outpaced fundamental progress. Over the past year, price increased 30% while TTM net income (~$6B) shows only modest growth, and the P/E ratio more than doubled from ~18x to ~39x.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Strong Q4 2024 revenue jump (+17.3% QoQ) with improved gross profit ($10.44B). Maintained annual dividend (implied by consistent equity growth).
- **Downside:** Highly volatile quarterly earnings, including a recent loss. Gross margin stable but operating margin compressed (Q3 2024 negative). Extreme valuation expansion (P/E 38.7) against poor earnings durability. High financial leverage (L/E 4.1x). Missing cash flow data obscures financial health.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative evidence presents a **cautionary** setup. IBM trades at a historically high valuation (P/E 38.7) while exhibiting poor earnings stability (recent quarterly net loss, volatile margins) and high leverage. The 30% annual price return appears disconnected from fundamental performance, which shows minimal revenue growth (YoY +1.0%) and deteriorating returns on equity (ROE dropping from 0.35 to 0.22). The lack of cash flow data is a significant analytical gap. The risk/reward is unfavorable at current levels given the valuation premium and weak recent earnings quality. Any reversion in valuation multiples would create significant downside, while upside depends on sustained revenue acceleration not yet evident in the trajectory.
IBM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: IBM (Decision Date: 2025-04-01)**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2025-02-25 10-K)**
IBM is a globally integrated technology company focused on the intersection of hybrid cloud and artificial intelligence (AI). Its strategy is to create value by helping clients with digital transformation, workflow reimagining, and application modernization. The business is structured into four segments:
*   **Software:** Provides hybrid cloud and AI platforms. Key competitors include Microsoft, Oracle, Salesforce, and SAP.
*   **Consulting:** Delivers business and technology consulting services. Competes with Accenture, Capgemini, and India-based service providers.
*   **Infrastructure:** Offers on-premises and cloud-based server and storage solutions for mission-critical and AI workloads. Competes with Dell, HPE, and cloud service providers.
*   **Financing:** Facilitates client acquisition of IBM's offerings. Competes with non-captive financial entities.
The company differentiates through its breadth of capabilities, deep industry expertise, research, and a broad partner ecosystem including AWS, Microsoft, and SAP.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Guidance & Outlook (2025-02-10, 2025-03-02, 2025-03-03):** Management raised 2025 growth targets, expecting revenue growth of about 5% (constant currency) and double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth, driven by portfolio mix, operating leverage, and productivity initiatives. Optimism is noted for a consulting recovery in the second half of 2025.
*   **Strategic Partnerships & Alliances (2025-03-19):** IBM is collaborating with NVIDIA, GE Healthcare, Google, and others as part of NVIDIA's new Accelerated Quantum Research Center to drive advancements in quantum computing and AI.
*   **Corporate Action & Policy (2025-03-13):** IBM dropped a corporate policy that considered diversity hiring in calculating executive pay, erasing related language from its annual report, citing legal challenges and White House pressure.
*   **Legal & Regulatory (2025-03-10, 2025-03-11):** The U.S. Supreme Court upheld an IBM victory in a $1.6 billion software contract dispute with BMC. Separately, a UK High Court ruled that a subsidiary of LzLabs reverse-engineered IBM software, a ruling described as posing an "existential threat" to LzLabs' business.
*   **Market Sentiment (2025-03-20):** IBM was cited in a "Top 5 Stocks to Buy" article, highlighting its strong financial position and ability to weather economic uncertainty.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and IP Threats.** The UK High Court ruling (2025-03-10) against LzLabs for reverse-engineering IBM software, while a win, underscores the ongoing threat to IBM's legacy mainframe business from modernization competitors. Concurrently, IBM faces ongoing overtime lawsuits (2025-02-20) from U.S. employees.
*   **Offset:** IBM successfully defended a major software contract in the U.S. Supreme Court (2025-03-11), reinforcing its legal position in key disputes.
*   **Risk: Intense Competition.** The 10-K (2025-02-25) details a highly competitive environment across all segments, with hundreds of competitors including large hyperscalers and niche players, which could pressure pricing and market share.
*   **Offset:** IBM's strategy leverages its hybrid cloud platform, deep incumbency with enterprises, and broad partner ecosystem to differentiate and compete.
*   **Risk: Execution in Growth Areas.** Success depends on driving growth in hybrid cloud and AI, and realizing a projected recovery in consulting. Market reports (e.g., 2025-03-21, 2025-03-24) indicate strong growth potential in these areas, but execution is not guaranteed.
*   **Offset:** Management has provided specific, raised guidance for 2025 (2025-02-10), indicating confidence in the growth trajectory and operational leverage.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The narrative is supported by management's raised 2025 guidance, strategic positioning in high-growth markets (hybrid cloud, AI), and recent legal victories. However, this is tempered by persistent competitive intensity, ongoing legal challenges, and the need to execute on a consulting recovery and technology leadership. The franchise remains relevant, but growth and margin expansion are not assured.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'QoQ: eps_diluted=-963.9% net_income=-983.0%' which are mechanically correct (from negative to positive EPS) but the report does not flag that these QoQ percentage changes are misleading artifacts of the sign change. The report uses these figures in the growth panel without caveat, which could mislead a reader into thinking there was a massive earnings decline rather than a swing from a loss quarter to a profitable one.
corrections.required_action: Note that QoQ EPS/net income percentage changes are arithmetic artifacts of the sign reversal (Q3 loss to Q4 profit) and should not be interpreted as directional declines. The narrative in the report does correctly describe the swing qualitatively, but the raw growth panel figures could be misread.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Net margin is 9.6%' and 'ROE from 0.351 to 0.22' but the raw evidence shows net_margin=0.096 and roe=0.22 at latest, with prior ROE at 0.351 (2024-05-02). The report also says 'net margin has compressed from 13.2% to 9.6%'. The raw evidence confirms net_margin went from 0.132 to 0.096, so this is consistent. However, the report states 'ROE dropping from 0.35 to 0.22' — the raw data shows roe=0.351 at 2024-05-02 and roe=0.22 at 2025-03-31, which is confirmed. No material issue here upon closer inspection.
corrections.required_action: No action needed — figures verified against raw evidence.

corrections.issue: The qual report references 'Market reports (e.g., 2025-03-21, 2025-03-24) indicate strong growth potential in these areas' — the raw qualitative evidence does include a 2025-03-21 hybrid cloud market report and a 2025-03-24 digital twin market report, but neither specifically references IBM's growth potential. These are general market forecasts, not IBM-specific catalysts. The qual report slightly overstates the specificity of these sources to IBM.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the cited market reports are general industry forecasts (hybrid cloud, digital twin markets) and do not specifically project IBM's growth. The connection to IBM is inferential, not direct.

unsupported_claims: : The quant report's QoQ EPS change of -963.9% is a sign-reversal artifact, not a meaningful directional metric. The report presents it in the growth panel without caveat, though the narrative text correctly describes the Q3 loss to Q4 profit swing qualitatively.

: The qual report states 'Market reports (e.g., 2025-03-21, 2025-03-24) indicate strong growth potential in these areas' — the supplied evidence for those dates are general market forecasts for hybrid cloud and digital twin markets, not IBM-specific projections. The inference is reasonable but the attribution overstates specificity.

IBM.verification:
**Recommendation**: KEEP
**Severity**: minor

**Verified points:**
- (none)

**Required corrections:**
- [C1] The quant report states 'QoQ: eps_diluted=-963.9% net_income=-983.0%' which are mechanically correct (from negative to positive EPS) but the report does not flag that these QoQ percentage changes are misleading artifacts of the sign change. The report uses these figures in the growth panel without caveat, which could mislead a reader into thinking there was a massive earnings decline rather than a swing from a loss quarter to a profitable one. → Note that QoQ EPS/net income percentage changes are arithmetic artifacts of the sign reversal (Q3 loss to Q4 profit) and should not be interpreted as directional declines. The narrative in the report does correctly describe the swing qualitatively, but the raw growth panel figures could be misread.
- [C2] The quant report states 'Net margin is 9.6%' and 'ROE from 0.351 to 0.22' but the raw evidence shows net_margin=0.096 and roe=0.22 at latest, with prior ROE at 0.351 (2024-05-02). The report also says 'net margin has compressed from 13.2% to 9.6%'. The raw evidence confirms net_margin went from 0.132 to 0.096, so this is consistent. However, the report states 'ROE dropping from 0.35 to 0.22' — the raw data shows roe=0.351 at 2024-05-02 and roe=0.22 at 2025-03-31, which is confirmed. No material issue here upon closer inspection. → No action needed — figures verified against raw evidence.
- [C3] The qual report references 'Market reports (e.g., 2025-03-21, 2025-03-24) indicate strong growth potential in these areas' — the raw qualitative evidence does include a 2025-03-21 hybrid cloud market report and a 2025-03-24 digital twin market report, but neither specifically references IBM's growth potential. These are general market forecasts, not IBM-specific catalysts. The qual report slightly overstates the specificity of these sources to IBM. → Clarify that the cited market reports are general industry forecasts (hybrid cloud, digital twin markets) and do not specifically project IBM's growth. The connection to IBM is inferential, not direct.

**Unsupported claims:**
- : The quant report's QoQ EPS change of -963.9% is a sign-reversal artifact, not a meaningful directional metric. The report presents it in the growth panel without caveat, though the narrative text correctly describes the Q3 loss to Q4 profit swing qualitatively.
- : The qual report states 'Market reports (e.g., 2025-03-21, 2025-03-24) indicate strong growth potential in these areas' — the supplied evidence for those dates are general market forecasts for hybrid cloud and digital twin markets, not IBM-specific projections. The inference is reasonable but the attribution overstates specificity.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: IBM's AI and hybrid cloud narrative remains compelling and management raised 2025 guidance, but the stock trades at a historically stretched P/E of ~39x against deteriorating earnings quality (net margin 9.6% vs. 13.2% a year ago, ROE compressed from 0.35 to 0.22) and a recent quarterly net loss. The risk-reward is roughly balanced at current levels—upside from execution on growth guidance is offset by valuation vulnerability if earnings disappoint.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: IBM's AI and hybrid cloud narrative remains compelling and management raised 2025 guidance, but the stock trades at a historically stretched P/E of ~39x against deteriorating earnings quality (net margin 9.6% vs. 13.2% a year ago, ROE compressed from 0.35 to 0.22) and a recent quarterly net loss. The risk-reward is roughly balanced at current levels—upside from execution on growth guidance is offset by valuation vulnerability if earnings disappoint.
key_factors: P/E of 38.7x is at the top of its 365-day range (18.8–40.7) and more than doubled from ~19x a year ago, while net margin compressed from 13.2% to 9.6% and ROE fell from 0.35 to 0.22—price appreciation has far outpaced fundamental progress.

Q4 2024 revenue of $17.55B (+17.3% QoQ) and management's raised 2025 guidance (~5% constant-currency revenue growth, double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth) signal genuine AI/hybrid cloud momentum, but Q3 2024 produced a net loss of -$330M and operating loss of -$802M, highlighting earnings volatility.
Balance sheet leverage remains high at 4.1x liabilities-to-equity ($109.78B / $27.39B), and missing cash flow data prevents assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion, adding analytical uncertainty.

Recent legal victories (U.S. Supreme Court upheld $1.6B software contract win; UK High Court ruled against LzLabs for reverse-engineering IBM software) and strategic partnerships (NVIDIA, GE Healthcare, Google) support the franchise, but intense competition from hyperscalers and niche players persists across all segments.

GENERAL
NIKE, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 under the laws of the State of Oregon. These patents expire at various times.
We believe our success depends upon our capabilities in areas such as design, research and development, production and marketing and is supported and protected by our intellectual property rights, such as trademarks, utility and design patents, copyrights, and trade secrets, among others.
We have followed a policy of applying for and registering intellectual property rights in the United States and select foreign countries on trademarks, inventions, innovations and designs that we deem protectable and valuable. We also continue to vigorously protect and enforce our intellectual property, including trademarks, patents and trade secrets against third-party infringement and misappropriation.
2024 FORM 10-K   5
Table of Contents
HUMAN CAPITAL RESOURCES
At NIKE, we consider the strength and effective management of our workforce to be essential to the ongoing success of our business. We believe that it is important to attract, develop and retain a diverse and engaged workforce at all levels of our business and that such a workforce fosters creativity and accelerates innovation. We are focused on building a talent pipeline that reflects our consumers, athletes and the communities we serve.
CULTURE
Each employee shapes NIKE's culture through behaviors and practices. This starts with our Maxims, which represent our core values and, along with our Code of Conduct, feature the fundamental behaviors that help anchor, inform and guide us and apply to all employees. Our mission is to bring inspiration and innovation to every athlete in the world, which includes the belief that if you have a body, you are an athlete. We aim to do this by creating groundbreaking sport innovations, making our products more sustainably, building a creative and diverse global team, supporting the well-being of our employees and making a positive impact in communities where we live and work. Our mission is aligned with our deep commitment to maintaining an environment where all NIKE employees have the opportunity to reach their full potential, to connect to our brands and to shape our workplace culture. We believe providing for growth and retention of our employees is essential in fostering such a culture and are dedicated to providing access to training programs and career development opportunities, including trainings on NIKE's values, history and business, trainings on developing leadership skills at all levels, tools and resources for managers and qualified tuition reimbursement opportunities.
In empowering our employees to help shape our culture, we source employee feedback through a variety of survey tools: our annual Engagement Survey program, corporate pulse surveys and listening sessions. These tools provide employees throughout the globe an opportunity to provide confidential feedback on key areas known to drive employee engagement, including their satisfaction with their managers, their work and the Company generally. These tools also measure our employees' connection to NIKE's culture. NIKE also provides multiple points of contact for employees to speak up if they experience something that does not align with our values or otherwise violates our workplace policies, even if they are uncertain what they observed or heard is a violation of company policy.
As part of our commitment to making a positive impact on our communities, we maintain a goal of investing 2% of our prior fiscal year's pre-tax income into global communities. The focus of this investment continues to be inspiring youth to be active through play and sport as well as uniting and inspiring communities to create a better and more equitable future for all. Our community investments are an important part of our culture, and we support employees in giving back to community organizations through volunteering and donations, which are matched by the NIKE Foundation where eligible.
EMPLOYEE BASE
As of May 31, 2024, we had approximately 79,400 employees worldwide, including retail and part-time employees. We also utilize independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce.
Most of our employees are not represented by unions, except for certain employees in the EMEA and APLA geographies who are members of and/or represented by trade unions, as allowed or required by local law and/or collective bargaining agreements. Also, in some countries outside of the United States, local laws require employee representation by works councils (which may be entitled to information and consultation on certain subsidiary decisions) or by organizations similar to a union. In certain European countries, we are required by local law to enter into, and/or comply with, industry-wide or national collective bargaining agreements. NIKE has never experienced a material interruption of operations due to labor disagreements.
DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND INCLUSION
Diversity, equity and inclusion ("DE&I") is a strategic priority for NIKE and we are committed to having an inclusive and diverse team and culture. We aim to foster an inclusive and accessible workplace through recruitment, development and retention of talent from diverse experiences and backgrounds with the goal of expanding representation across all dimensions of diversity over the long term. We remain committed to the targets announced in fiscal 2021 for the Company to work toward by fiscal 2025, including diverse representation in our corporate workforce and leadership positions.
We continue our efforts to recruit talent through our traditional channels and through initiatives, such as partnerships with athletes and sports-related organizations to create apprenticeship programs and new partnerships with organizations, colleges and universities that serve diverse populations. Additionally, we are prioritizing DE&I education so that all NIKE employees and leaders have the cultural knowledge and understanding to lead inclusively and build diverse and inclusive teams. We also have Employee Networks, collectively known as NikeUNITED, representing various employee groups.

We have committed to investments that aim to address racial inequality and improve diversity and representation in our communities. We also are leveraging our global scale to support business diversity among the businesses with which we work.
COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS
